issue_body:
`DOTNET_ROOT` is used to resolve ASP.NET Core folders in two places:

https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/blob/5bd03bbf50d4e209ec9119a0e98f2da9a247c766/src/Framework/test/TargetingPackTests.cs#L28-L32

https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/blob/5bd03bbf50d4e209ec9119a0e98f2da9a247c766/src/Framework/test/SharedFxTests.cs#L27-L31

What is `DOTNET_ROOT` supposed to be set to?
Can it be the root of the the dotnet installation that is used for the build?
Or should it be set to something that contains the ASP.NET Core bits that got build? To what?

I've currently set it to the first (`<repo>/.dotnet`), and I get test failures for:
```
Microsoft.AspNetCore.SharedFxTests.SharedFrameworkContainsExpectedFiles
Microsoft.AspNetCore.SharedFxTests.SharedFrameworkContainsListedAssemblies
Microsoft.AspNetCore.SharedFxTests.RuntimeListContainsCorrectEntries
Microsoft.AspNetCore.TargetingPackTests.PlatformManifestListsAllFiles
```
which look like:
```
Xunit.Sdk.EmptyException: Assert.Empty() Failure\nExpected: <empty>\nActual:   [\"System.Diagnostics.EventLog.Messages\"]
at Microsoft.AspNetCore.SharedFxTests.SharedFrameworkContainsExpectedFiles() in /_/src/Framework/test/SharedFxTests.cs:line 81
```

My build uses these two commands:
```
eng/build.sh --ci --arch x64 -c Release --restore --build --pack
eng/build.sh --ci --arch x64 -c Release --test
```

@dougbu am I using the proper value for `DOTNET_ROOT`?

cc @omajid
pr_title: Framework.Tests: support running against Linux build artifacts.
pr_body:
Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/46524.

@dougbu ptal.

These are the changes that make the tests pass for a local build.

Can we make it so that the aspnetcore CI when running on X will consume the binaries built for X?
Or will CI keep on running these tests against Windows build artifacts only?

In what files should I make the changes for `HELIX_WORKITEM_ROOT`?

cc @omajid
